en: If your company performs any of the following services and is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) for those services, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.

In Japan, the Certificate of Incorporation and the Certificate of Seal-Impression are essential documents used to verify a company’s legal registration and its officially authorized seal, which functions as the company’s binding signature. These certificates are required to confirm a business’s legitimate existence and its authority to enter contracts, conduct transactions, and complete formal compliance processes.
Does your company perform work in Japan and maintain business entity certificates?
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing business entity certificates in Japan.
The company is a sole proprietorship with no other employees.
The company is a foreign company which is not registered and does not have a legal presence in Japan.
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing business entity certificates in Japan.
Please select the business entity certificates your company maintains.
